page: Microsoft Azure D8ads_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dadv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor, operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache per core. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It includes a 472 GB local storage disk and supports Premium Storage, but does not feature any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the D8ads_v6 exhibits strong single-core capabilities, with its stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 25%. Text processing performance is particularly notable, ranking in the top 10% of tested systems. Multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations generally align with average mid-tier expectations. For instance, Redis SET operations reach 11.53 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput is measured at 12.28 GB/sec. However, a performance tradeoff is evident in complex workloads; while small-model LLM inference is average, prompt processing for a medium 7B model falls into the bottom 25%.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D8ads_v6 offers a balanced resource density suitable for general-purpose workloads that require a mix of compute, memory, and local storage. The inclusion of a 472 GB local disk and Premium Storage capability provides a cost-effective option for workloads requiring fast temporary storage without needing dedicated storage-optimized hardware. This configuration is well-suited for applications such as web serving, software compilation, text processing, and moderate database operations, though it is less optimal for heavy multi-threaded parallel computing or large-scale machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D8ads_v6 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 32.0 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The server includes 472 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it lacks GPU accelerators. Benchmarks reveal strong single-core performance, with Geekbench single-core and stress-ng single-core scores in the top 25%, and text processing in the top 10%. Conversely, multi-core operations, memory bandwidth, and database tasks perform at average levels, while medium-model LLM prompt processing shows weaker relative performance. This resource profile makes the D8ads_v6 qualitatively cost-efficient for general-purpose workloads, including web serving, software compilation, and moderate database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D8ads_v6 is an x86_64 general-purpose VM featuring 8 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 472 GB local storage, delivering strong single-core performance for web serving and database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D8ads_v6 is a general-purpose VM with 8 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 472 GB local storage, optimized for balanced workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, integrated local storage, and strong single-core performance.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 32 GB RAM
Includes 472 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Delivers strong single-core CPU and top-tier text processing performance
Maintains average multi-core, memory bandwidth, and database throughput
Suited for web serving, software compilation, and moderate database workloads
